mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in the unemployment rate below 4.0% signals tight labor market conditions that pressure wage growth and trigger Federal Reserve rate hikes, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE falls below 4.0%, or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns weakens to |rho| < 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in residential construction (HOUST) and consumer spending (RSAFS) indicates strong demand-side momentum that outpaces corporate profit growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of periods where both HOUST and RSAFS rise by > 2% YoY, or the interaction term between HOUST and RSAFS loses statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening supply-side capacity utilization that reduces corporate earnings visibility, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of instances where INDPRO declines by > 1.5% MoM, or the coefficient on INDPRO in regression models falls below |β| < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 150k signals robust labor market dynamics that support consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 40% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 150k, or the predictive power of PAYEMS in out-of-sample tests drops below Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the CPI inflation rate above 2.5% signals central bank policy inaction that erodes real interest rates and drives equity valuations higher, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL and SPX returns weakens to |rho| < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized expansion in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong macroeconomic momentum that outpaces market expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 45% of periods where both GDPC1 and HOUST rise by > 1% QoQ, or the interaction term between GDPC1 and HOUST loses significance (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) combined with rising energy prices (CPIAUCSL) indicates a supply-side shock that increases corporate costs and reduces earnings visibility, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO falls below 110 and CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.0%, or the interaction term between INDPRO and CPIAUCSL loses significance (p > 0.10).
